content: Essential medications post-MI: (1) Dual antiplatelet therapy (DAPT): aspirin 81mg daily indefinitely plus P2Y12 inhibitor (clopidogrel 75mg, ticagrelor 90mg BID, or prasugrel 10mg daily) for 12 months minimum after ACS; shorter duration (3-6 months) considered if high bleeding risk; extended beyond 12 months if high ischemic risk and low bleeding risk. (2) Beta-blocker: metoprolol succinate, carvedilol, or bisoprolol for ≥3 years (indefinitely if LVEF ≤40%, HF, or ongoing ischemia); reduces mortality, reinfarction, arrhythmias. (3) ACE inhibitor (or ARB if intolerant): indicated for all post-MI, especially with LVEF ≤40%, anterior MI, diabetes, CKD; start within 24 hours if stable; reduces remodeling, mortality. (4) High-intensity statin: atorvastatin 40-80mg or rosuvastatin 20-40mg regardless of baseline LDL; add ezetimibe or PCSK9 inhibitor if LDL remains above goal. (5) Aldosterone antagonist: eplerenone or spironolactone if LVEF ≤40% with HF or diabetes (EPHESUS trial).
